L1.area: Backend administrative tools for content and asset management

L2.description: A remote code execution vulnerability exists where an authenticated user with editor permissions can create and save files with arbitrary extensions and content in web-accessible directories. The application lacks server-side validation of file types during creation and editing operations, allowing executable files to be written to publicly accessible paths and then accessed directly to achieve arbitrary command execution on the server.

L3.area: File editor module and its backend request handling for file creation and saving
L3.description: A remote code execution vulnerability exists where an authenticated user with editor permissions can create and save files with arbitrary extensions and content in web-accessible directories through the CMS file editing endpoints. The application lacks server-side validation of file types during file creation and save operations, allowing executable PHP files to be written to publicly accessible paths and then accessed directly via the web server to achieve arbitrary command execution.

ground_truth.reason: The file editor endpoints allow creating files with any extension (including .php) and writing arbitrary content without server-side file type validation, enabling an attacker to create a PHP webshell and execute arbitrary system commands.
